title: dipsacus sylvestris subsp. laciniatus
intro: Dipsacus sylvestris subsp. laciniatus, also known as the cut-leaved teasel, is a perennial plant species in the teasel family (Dipsacaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dipsacus sylvestris subsp. laciniatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Dipsacus sylvestris subsp. laciniatus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
